quizQuestion: On which website did the Rickroll meme originate in May 2007?
explanation: The Rickroll originated on 4chan when user Shawn Cotter posted a link claiming to be a Grand Theft Auto IV trailer that instead played Rick Astley's 'Never Gonna Give You Up.' The bait-and-switch trick is still active 18+ years later. Source: Wikipedia/Rickrolling

quizQuestion: What was the username of the 4chan user credited with starting the Rickroll meme in 2007?
explanation: The Rickroll was started on 4chan by a user named Shawn Cotter, who posted a deceptive link claiming to lead to a Grand Theft Auto IV trailer. The prank spread worldwide and remains a beloved internet tradition. Source: Wikipedia/Rickrolling

quizQuestion: Who created the Nyan Cat animation in April 2011?
explanation: Christopher Torres created Nyan Cat in April 2011 — a pixelated cat with a Pop-Tart body flying through space with a rainbow trail. It became the 5th most viewed YouTube video of 2011. Source: Wikipedia/Nyan Cat

quizQuestion: In what year did Nyan Cat become the 5th most viewed YouTube video?
explanation: Nyan Cat was created and went viral in April 2011, finishing the year as the 5th most-viewed video on YouTube. The looping Pop-Tart cat with a rainbow trail and catchy soundtrack captivated millions worldwide. Source: Wikipedia/Nyan Cat

quizQuestion: In what year was the original Keyboard Cat footage recorded on VHS?
explanation: The original Keyboard Cat video was recorded on VHS in 1984, featuring a cat named Fatso being held upright and 'playing' a keyboard. The footage was uploaded to YouTube in 2007 and went viral in 2009, long after the cat had already died in 1987. Source: Wikipedia/Keyboard Cat

quizQuestion: What was the name of the cat in the original Keyboard Cat footage filmed in 1984?
explanation: The original Keyboard Cat was a cat named Fatso, owned by Charlie Schmidt. The footage was shot on VHS in 1984, but Fatso died in 1987 — making the beloved internet cat famous more than 20 years after its death. Source: Wikipedia/Keyboard Cat

quizQuestion: What was the name of the Shiba Inu whose photo became the Doge meme?
explanation: The Doge meme features a Shiba Inu named Kabosu, whose photo went viral in February 2010. Kabosu's image became one of the internet's most recognizable memes, later inspiring Dogecoin in December 2013. Kabosu passed away in May 2024. Source: Wikipedia/Doge meme

quizQuestion: In what year was Dogecoin launched, inspired by the Doge meme?
explanation: Dogecoin was launched in December 2013, inspired by the Doge Shiba Inu meme that had gone viral in 2010. The cryptocurrency was initially created as a joke but eventually reached a market cap of over $80 billion at its peak in 2021. Source: Wikipedia/Doge meme

quizQuestion: Which artist created Pepe the Frog in his 2005 webcomic?
explanation: Matt Furie created Pepe the Frog in his 2005 webcomic 'Boy's Club.' The 'Feels good man' frog went viral on 4chan from 2008 onward. Furie has spent years trying to reclaim Pepe as a symbol of positivity. Source: Wikipedia/Pepe the Frog

quizQuestion: What was the name of Matt Furie's webcomic where Pepe the Frog first appeared in 2005?
explanation: Pepe the Frog debuted in Matt Furie's 2005 webcomic 'Boy's Club,' originally as a laid-back character whose catchphrase was 'Feels good man.' The character was later widely appropriated as a meme, beginning around 2008. Source: Wikipedia/Pepe the Frog

quizQuestion: Approximately how many million views did 'Charlie Bit My Finger' accumulate on YouTube?
explanation: 'Charlie Bit My Finger,' uploaded in May 2007, amassed 888+ million views, making it the #1 ranked video in TIME's list of YouTube's 50 greatest viral videos. The original was later removed and sold as an NFT for $760,999 in 2021. Source: Wikipedia/Charlie Bit My Finger

quizQuestion: For how many dollars was the 'Charlie Bit My Finger' video sold as an NFT in 2021?
explanation: 'Charlie Bit My Finger' was sold as an NFT for $760,999 in 2021, after which the original was removed from YouTube. The video, uploaded in May 2007, had accumulated 888 million views and was considered a landmark of early internet culture. Source: Wikipedia/Charlie Bit My Finger

quizQuestion: In what year did Gangnam Style become the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views?
explanation: Gangnam Style by PSY, released in July 2012, became the first YouTube video to reach 1 billion views in December 2012. It was a turning point for K-pop's global visibility and now has over 5 billion views. Source: Wikipedia/Gangnam Style

quizQuestion: Which South Korean rapper performed the globally viral Gangnam Style music video in 2012?
explanation: PSY (Park Jae-sang) released Gangnam Style in July 2012. The song and its accompanying horse-riding dance became a global phenomenon and a turning point for K-pop's international reach. Source: Wikipedia/Gangnam Style

quizQuestion: In what year did Baby Shark become the first YouTube video to reach 10 billion views?
explanation: Baby Shark by Pinkfong reached 10 billion views in January 2022, making it the most-viewed video in YouTube history. Originally uploaded in June 2016, it now has over 16 billion views and holds the Guinness World Record. Source: Wikipedia/Baby Shark

quizQuestion: Which South Korean entertainment company uploaded Baby Shark to YouTube in 2016?
explanation: Baby Shark was uploaded by Pinkfong, a South Korean education and entertainment brand, in June 2016. The video became the most-viewed video in YouTube history, surpassing Gangnam Style to reach 10 billion views in January 2022. Source: Wikipedia/Baby Shark

quizQuestion: On which platform did Gary Brolsma originally post his Numa Numa lip-sync video in December 2004?
explanation: Gary Brolsma posted his lip-sync to 'Dragostea Din Tei' by O-Zone on Newgrounds in December 2004. The video accumulated hundreds of millions of views and is considered one of the first great viral internet videos. Source: Wikipedia/Numa Numa

quizQuestion: In what year did Mark Zuckerberg launch Facebook from his Harvard dorm room?
explanation: Mark Zuckerberg launched Facebook on February 4, 2004, from his Harvard dormitory. The site reached 1 million users by the end of 2004 and received its first major investment — $500,000 from Peter Thiel — that same year. Source: Wikipedia/Facebook

quizQuestion: In what year did Jack Dorsey send the first tweet, 'just setting up my twttr'?
explanation: Jack Dorsey sent the first tweet — 'just setting up my twttr' — on March 21, 2006. Twitter then launched publicly on July 15, 2006. The platform became a global venue for breaking news and cultural commentary. Source: Wikipedia/Twitter

quizQuestion: In what year did ByteDance launch TikTok for international audiences outside China?
explanation: ByteDance launched TikTok globally in September 2017, separate from its Chinese version Douyin (launched September 2016). ByteDance then acquired Musical.ly for approximately $1 billion in November 2017 and merged it into TikTok in 2018. Source: Wikipedia/TikTok

quizQuestion: Who co-founded Instagram alongside Mike Krieger in October 2010?
explanation: Kevin Systrom co-founded Instagram with Mike Krieger, launching the app on October 6, 2010. Instagram gained 25,000 users on its first day, reached 1 million users in under three months, and was acquired by Facebook for $1 billion in 2012. Source: Wikipedia/Instagram

quizQuestion: For how many billion dollars did Facebook acquire Instagram in 2012?
explanation: Facebook acquired Instagram for $1 billion in 2012, just 18 months after the app launched. At the time, Instagram had about 30 million users and no revenue — the price shocked the tech world. Source: Wikipedia/Instagram

quizQuestion: Who uploaded 'Me at the zoo,' the first video ever posted on YouTube?
explanation: Jawed Karim, a YouTube co-founder, uploaded 'Me at the zoo' on April 23, 2005. The 19-second clip filmed at the San Diego Zoo has over 385 million views and is preserved as a landmark of internet history. Source: Wikipedia/Me at the zoo

quizQuestion: In what year was 'Me at the zoo,' the first YouTube video, uploaded?
explanation: Jawed Karim uploaded 'Me at the zoo' on April 23, 2005, marking the beginning of YouTube's public history. The 19-second clip was filmed at the San Diego Zoo and has since accumulated over 385 million views. Source: Wikipedia/Me at the zoo

quizQuestion: Who co-founded Wikipedia alongside Larry Sanger in January 2001?
explanation: Jimmy Wales and Larry Sanger co-founded Wikipedia on January 15, 2001. It was preceded by Nupedia, a more traditional expert-reviewed encyclopedia launched in March 2000. Wikipedia grew to become the largest reference site in human history. Source: Wikipedia/Wikipedia

quizQuestion: Who co-founded Reddit alongside Alexis Ohanian in June 2005?
explanation: Steve Huffman and Alexis Ohanian co-founded Reddit in June 2005. The site was sold to Condé Nast in October 2006 for an estimated $10-20 million. Reddit became one of the most visited websites in the world, known as 'the front page of the internet.' Source: Wikipedia/Reddit

quizQuestion: In what year did Jason Citron and Stanislav Vishnevskiy launch Discord?
explanation: Jason Citron and Stanislav Vishnevskiy launched Discord in May 2015, originally designed for gamers to communicate during play sessions. Discord grew far beyond gaming to become a major platform for communities of all kinds. Source: Wikipedia/Discord

quizQuestion: What were the actual colors of the dress at the center of the viral 2015 'The Dress' debate?
explanation: The famous dress from the February 25, 2015 viral debate was actually blue and black, though millions perceived it as white and gold due to differences in color perception and ambient light interpretation. The debate generated over 2.2 million tweets in under 24 hours. Source: Wikipedia/The dress

quizQuestion: Approximately how many million dollars did the Ice Bucket Challenge raise for ALS research in 2014?
explanation: The Ice Bucket Challenge, popularized in July-August 2014 by Pete Frates and Pat Quinn, raised over $115 million for ALS (amyotrophic lateral sclerosis) research. The challenge generated 1.2 million Facebook videos and became a landmark of social media fundraising. Source: Wikipedia/Ice Bucket Challenge

quizQuestion: In what year did The New York Times acquire the word game Wordle from its creator?
explanation: The New York Times acquired Wordle from creator Josh Wardle in January 2022 for a 'seven-figure sum.' Wordle had grown from 90 players when it launched publicly in October 2021 to over 2 million players in just two months. Source: Wikipedia/Wordle

quizQuestion: Which Vietnamese developer created Flappy Bird before removing it from app stores in February 2014?
explanation: Dong Nguyen created Flappy Bird in May 2013 under his studio .GEARS. The game reached 50 million downloads and generated $50,000 per day in ad revenue before Nguyen famously removed it from app stores in February 2014, saying it was 'too addictive.' Source: Wikipedia/Flappy Bird
